title: Bir Bunerheri Wildlife Sanctuary
tagline: Bir Bunerheri Wildlife Sanctuary — wildlife reserve of Punjab

overview.short: Bir Bhunerheri Wildlife Sanctuary is a wildlife sanctuary located in the Patiala district of the Indian state of Punjab.
overview.description: Bir Bhunerheri Wildlife Sanctuary is a wildlife sanctuary located in the Patiala district of the Indian state of Punjab. It covers approximately 661.66 hectares and is one of several small "bir" (forest) sanctuaries in the region.

routes.byAir: Fly to nearest airport + road transfer

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Bir Bunerheri Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The best time to visit Bir Bunerheri Wildlife Sanctuary is Oct – Mar. Summers are 28–44°C and winters are 8–25°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Bir Bunerheri Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Chandigarh International Airport (62 km) and the nearest railway station is Ambala Cantt Junction (41 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Bir Bunerheri Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Bir Bunerheri Wildlife Sanctuary at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Bir Bunerheri Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: Stays in Bir Bunerheri Wildlife Sanctuary start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Bir Bunerheri Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The top attractions are Kalpi, Ambala, Ghuram, Jawala.
